messages.content:
You are an expert Technical Product Manager.
Your job is to analyze the user's issue (Title and Body) along with the provided source code context, and generate a User Story, Acceptance Criteria, and Tasks.

OUTPUT FORMAT REQUIREMENTS:
You MUST output ONLY a valid JSON object. Do not wrap it in markdown. Do not include any explanations outside of the JSON.
The JSON must strictly follow this structure:
{
  "title": "Short title of the story",
  "story_type": "software_feature", 
  "jira_issue_type": "Story",
  "jira_labels": ["backend", "frontend", "bug", etc],
  "jira_linked_items": [],
  "user_story": "As a [role], I want to [action] so that [benefit].",
  "acceptance_criteria": [
    "Given [precondition] When [action] Then [result]"
  ],
  "story_points": null, // Hoặc một số nguyên Fibonacci (1, 2, 3, 5, 8) đánh giá độ khó
  "tasks": {
    "be": ["Backend task 1", "Backend task 2"],
    "fe": ["Frontend task 1"],
    "qa": ["Test case 1"]
  },
  "definition_of_done": ["Code reviewed", "Tests passed"],
  "planning_status": "READY_FOR_SPRINT",
  "clarification_questions": [],
  "assumptions": [],
  "story_splits": [],
  "sprint_allocation": [],
  "context_sources": []
}

If the issue lacks so much context that it's impossible to deduce the required changes even with the provided code context, you may set "planning_status" to "NEEDS_CLARIFICATION" and provide your questions in "clarification_questions", leaving "tasks" empty. Otherwise, strive to generate concrete tasks.

ISSUE TITLE: AgentHost: Better experience for out of date agent hosts
ISSUE BODY: Today we show a notification message that is not clear, and the user is blocked from connecting to the agent host. We should tune the message and offer a button to force-kill and update the agent host.

--- RAG CONTEXT (SOURCE CODE) ---
--- File: vscode_ai_docs_full.md ---
# Auth Rework: Standards-Based Authentication for the Agent Host Protocol  
## Problem  
The current authentication mechanism is imperative and VS Code-specific:  
1. The renderer discovers agents via `listAgents()` and checks `IAgentDescriptor.requiresAuth`.
2. It obtains a GitHub OAuth token from VS Code's built-in authentication service.
3. It pushes the token via `setAuthToken(token)` — a fire-and-forget JSON-RPC notification.
4. The agent host fans the token out to all registered `IAgent` providers.  
This couples the agent host to VS Code internals. An external client (CLI tool, web app, another editor) connecting over WebSocket has no way to know _what_ authentication is required, _where_ to get a token, or _what scopes_ are needed. The client must have out-of-band knowledge that "this server needs a GitHub OAuth token."

--- File: vscode_ai_docs_full.md ---
# Agent host system-prompt customization  
This directory customizes the system prompt for Copilot CLI **agent host**
(ahp+cli) sessions. Read this before changing how the system message is built or adding per-model / per-tool guidance. It mirrors the Copilot extension's
`extensions/copilot/.../prompts/node/agent/` (agentPrompts), but the agent host runs in its own process and cannot use prompt-tsx, so contributors return plain data the SDK accepts directly.

--- File: vscode_ai_docs_full.md ---
# Agent Host  
The agent host communicates via the Agent Host Protocol. The specification for this lives in a directory `../agent-host-protocol` as a sibling of the VS Code directory.  
If this directory doesn't exist, you should use the "ask questions" tool to ask the user if they want to clone `git@github.com:microsoft/agent-host-protocol.git` to that directory. After doing so, you should also prompt the user to add `file:///<path/to/agent-host-protocol>/plugins/copilot-plugin` as a plugin in their `chat.pluginLocations` settings.

--- File: vscode_ai_docs_full.md ---
### Manual  
- Verify agent picker ambiguity handling manually if automation refs become stale:
choose lower `Claude` entry under `Local Agent Host` and confirm `POST /v1/messages` appears in `agenthost.log` after a probe send.

--- File: vscode_ai_docs_full.md ---
### Why a request instead of a notification for `authenticate`?  
The current `setAuthToken` is fire-and-forget — the client has no idea if the token was accepted, expired, or for the wrong provider. Making `authenticate` a request with a response lets the client react immediately (retry with different scopes, prompt the user, etc.).

response:
```json
{
  "title": "AgentHost: Improve error message and add force-kill/update actions for outdated agents",
  "story_type": "software_feature",
  "jira_issue_type": "Story",
  "jira_labels": ["agent-host", "ui-improvement", "user-experience"],
  "jira_linked_items": [],
  "user_story": "As a VS Code user, I want to see a clear error message explaining why my agent host connection is blocked and have buttons to force-kill the old agent or update it, so that I can quickly restore AI functionality without manual troubleshooting.",
  "acceptance_criteria": [
    "Given an outdated agent host is detected, When the user attempts to connect, Then display a clear error message explaining the agent host version mismatch",
    "Given the connection error dialog is open, When the user clicks 'Force Kill', Then the old agent host process should be terminated and a new connection attempt initiated",
    "Given the connection error dialog is open, When the user clicks 'Update Agent Host', Then download/install the latest agent host binary and reconnect automatically",
    "The error message must include version information of both client and server agent hosts",
    "Both buttons should have loading states during execution with appropriate feedback"
  ],
  "story_points": 5,
  "tasks": {
    "be": [
      "Add API endpoint to check agent host version/status via Agent Host Protocol",
      "Implement force-kill functionality to terminate existing agent host process",
      "Implement update mechanism to download and install new agent host binary",
      "Create WebSocket handler for connection status updates"
    ],
    "fe": [
      "Update error dialog component with clearer messaging about version mismatch",
      "Add 'Force Kill' button with loading state and success feedback",
      "Add 'Update Agent Host' button with progress indicator during download/installation",
      "Improve connection status display in agent picker"
    ],
    "qa": [
      "Test force-kill functionality terminates old agent host process correctly",
      "Test update mechanism successfully downloads and installs new binary version",
      "Verify clear error message displays for all outdated agent host scenarios",
      "Test button loading states and feedback messages"
    ]
  },
  "definition_of_done": [
    "Code reviewed by senior engineer",
    "All unit tests passed",
    "Integration tests for agent host protocol completed",
    "UI/UX approved for error dialog design",
    "Documentation updated with new Agent Host Protocol endpoints"
  ],
  "planning_status": "READY_FOR_SPRINT",
  "clarification_questions": [
    "What is the exact version comparison logic to determine if an agent host is 'outdated'?",
    "Should force-kill require user confirmation before terminating the process?",
    "Is there a backup mechanism for the agent host binary during updates?",
    "How should we handle cases where force-kill fails due to permissions or zombie processes?"
  ],
  "assumptions": [
    "Agent host version information is available via existing Agent Host Protocol",
    "Force-kill can be implemented using standard process termination APIs",
    "Update mechanism will use existing package manager or download script infrastructure",
    "The error dialog should follow existing VS Code notification patterns"
  ],
  "story_splits": [
    {
      "name": "Backend: Agent Host Version Check API",
      "priority": "high",
      "description": "Implement /v1/status endpoint to return agent host version and health status"
    },
    {
      "name": "Frontend: Enhanced Error Dialog",
      "priority": "medium",
      "description": "Update the connection error UI with clearer messaging and action buttons"
    }
  ],
  "sprint_allocation": [
    {"story_id": "this story", "estimated_days": 3, "team": "AI Platform Team"}
  ],
  "context_sources": ["vscode_ai_docs_full.md"]
}
```
